title: Insert Alignment ELYRA7
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"85b8i","text":"Alignment of the stage insert to ensure that the sample is perpendicular to the optical axis. This protocol is based on material provided by ZEISS' application specialists. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"daoke","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2lm0g","text":"What is needed before to start:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3bpkm","text":"    - This protocol was designed for the ELYRA7","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7bd4","text":"    - You will need 2 microscopy slides, one of standard size and one of about 2x2cm","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3pb0s","text":"  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: This protocol assumes that the microscope is on, and ready for operation ZEN black -> Locate Tab, View of Tabs on ZEN black, top left side and set an imaging protocol for transmitted light so you can see the sample via the eye pieces. Microscope Control example for transmission ZEN black 3.0 SR FP2 relea ZEISS
Change the objective to the lowest magnification, typically 10X You can change objectives in the Microscope Control panel
Place the marker-slide on the sample holder so you can focus on the appropriate sample plane Marker-slide on the slide holder
Focus on the sample via the eye pieces Marker spot in focus as viewed via the eye pieces
Remove the sample holder carefully and place it a side
Remove the objective lens and carefully place it a side

Place the 2x2cm clean glass plate on the objective turret. To avoid adding fingerprints to the slide you can wear protective gloves. Make sure not to touch the cupper pin with with glass. This pin is used to read the objectives collar, and it is delicate.
Place back the sample holder (which still has on it the marker sample)
Go to ZEN black -> Acquisition Tab
Set the aquisiton settings
Load the acquisiton settings by using the Experiment Manager In our case these settings are stored as "insert alignment"
While in Live mode , carefully take out the fibre from the HXP that is connected to microscope body until you see the sharp edges of the fibre in the field of view
Go to Live mode and make sure image is not saturated
Use the levelling screws of the holder to match the 2 sets of reflections to one another
Take an image snap , and save the image for your own record
Remove the sample holder carefully so you don't touch/move the levelling screws From now on be very careful of not moving the levelling screws
Remove the glass slide placed at the base of the objective port
Place back the objective
Place back the sample holder Be careful not to touch the levelling screws and not to hit the objective lens with the holder.
Close the imaging chamber
Go to Channels , and set the exposure time to a short value, such as 10ms, make sure not to over expose your camera.

Prepare a clean microscopy slide having a few marker points, marker-slide . You can keep track of the surface on which you made the marks by writing TOP and DOWN The slide can be reused Example of marker-slide used to move to find the sample plane by focusing with the low magnification objective
Prepare a clean microscopy slide of size aproximately 2x2cm Small slide can be made by cutting a larger slide into the appropiate size

Example imaging setup : Turn on the reflected light lamp at low intensity (2%), place the multiple bandpass dichroic (BP 420-480 + BP 495-550 + LP 655), select camera TV1, use tube lens / optovar 1x and select emission filter SBS LP 560
go to step #14 and select you have settings
Save the current experimental settings via the Experiment Manager
Find where the fibre of the HXP is connected to the microscope body. This will be on the left hand side of the microscope's body, after the on/off button
Place back the HXP fibre into position so you do not see the edges of the fibre while in live view
Turn on the ELYRA 7
Turn on Main Switch
Turn on Components
Turn on the Microscope Computer In the ELYRA 7 if the microscope computer is not on, then the microscope's touch screen will not light up.
Open ZEN black , and choose start system
creator: Rafael Camacho
